beats.activity: Wake up, brew coffee, check sourdough starter and check on plants.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; sourdough needs attention after overnight proof, and Raindrop and other houseplants are part of morning routine. Clear, hot day ahead changes the energy from yesterday's rain.

beats.activity: Commute to First Hill office via bus.
beats.reasoning: Tax season continues; early start to beat heat and crowds on the bus.

beats.activity: Morning and mid-morning tax work at office; morning client calls and file prep.
beats.reasoning: Tax season workload; no distraction today like yesterday's World Cup—Raj focuses on files and client work.

beats.activity: Lunch at a new Vietnamese spot near First Hill.
beats.reasoning: Adventurous eater; repeating favorite after yesterday's success, staying hydrated and fueled before afternoon heat.

beats.activity: Afternoon tax season work; client meetings and compliance review.
beats.reasoning: Stays at office through peak heat (91F); air-conditioned workspace preferable to commuting in smoke and high temperature.

beats.activity: Commute home via bus; beat the evening crowd.
beats.reasoning: Leaves office before peak rush; bus ride gives time to decompress before evening.

beats.activity: Quick stop at Whole Foods Market for fresh herbs and ingredients for dinner; grab water.
beats.reasoning: En route to Gas Works Park; picks up supplies for evening meal, stays hydrated given heat and smoke.

beats.activity: Head to Gas Works Park for outdoor concert; early arrival to secure a good spot before crowds.
beats.reasoning: Clear, warm evening perfect for outdoor concert; early arrival avoids big crowds (values close friends/smaller gatherings over masses, but concert at ideal time). Brings water bottle given smoke and heat.

beats.activity: Enjoy outdoor concert at Gas Works Park; live music and evening air.
beats.reasoning: Perfect weather window; concert runs through early evening. Raj enjoys the vibe, good for mood after tax season grind.

beats.activity: Walk home from Gas Works Park along the waterfront.
beats.reasoning: Pleasant evening temperature; walkable distance from park to home via scenic route, no need for transit.

beats.activity: Prepare simple dinner with fresh herbs; photograph the sourdough loaf; light film review.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down after concert; sourdough bake complete, captures it with camera before eating. Relaxes with film negatives from last weekend's hike.

beats.activity: Sleep; cool down after hot day.
beats.reasoning: Seven-hour sleep; overnight rest after active evening, ready for next tax season day.

diary: Started the day with my usual ritual—coffee and checking on the sourdough starter, which is bubbling nicely. The plants looked thirsty in this heat, so I gave them some attention before heading to the office. Tax season keeps me tied to my desk, but today the work felt manageable. Grabbed a bowl at Phở Hòa during lunch, this new spot near First Hill—the broth was solid. By mid-afternoon I was done, so I beat the rush home and swung by Whole Foods for fresh basil and cilantro. The real highlight was heading to Gas Works Park early for that free concert. I claimed a decent spot on the grass before the crowds rolled in, and watching the sun soften over the water while the music played felt like exactly what I needed. Even with the wildfire haze hanging around, the evening was perfect. Walked home along the waterfront afterward, made a simple dinner with those herbs, and actually photographed the sourdough loaf before eating it. Felt grounded.
thought_process: The heat today made me think differently about my afternoon—usually I'd just head home and crash after tax work, but ninety-one degrees and clear skies felt like a gift I shouldn't waste sitting indoors. I deliberately left work early, grabbed ingredients for something fresh and light, and committed to the concert. The wildfire smoke bothered me a bit (I'm usually a hiker, so air quality matters), but it was mild enough that I didn't let it stop me. There's something about being fifty that makes me more intentional with these moments. The day's rhythm felt less like obligation and more like choices—the work fed the ability to enjoy the evening, and the evening reminded me why I don't want to spend all my time at a desk.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
